title: enteropogon prieurii
intro: Enteropogon prieurii, also known as Pigeon Grass or Kera, is a type of grass native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Enteropogon prieurii include reducing fever, treating skin irritations, and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, or as a decoction by boiling the roots in water, to harness its medicinal properties.
